Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To The Office Of The Controller, Whistleblower Program
Off Calendar Jun-14-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To The Office Of The Controller, Whistleblower Program.

Defendant's motion to quash is granted. The whistleblower information is confidential and the court must balance the interests of the parties and the public to determine whether disclosure is proper. (See Evidence Code sec. 1040(b)(2).) That balance weighs in defendant's favor. Confidentiality is necessary to preserve participation in the whistleblower program. In this case, it is unnecessary to produce the requested information because: (1) plaintiff was not disciplined and did not suffer any adverse employment action due to the allegedly bogus whistleblower complaint; and (2) there is no authority that sitting for an investigation alone is an adverse employment action. (See Darling Decl., par. 3.) Since the investigation did not result in an adverse employment action, the sought information does little to further plaintiff's claims.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order that repeates verbatim the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
